macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady Amid Iran War Risks
items.summary: The Federal Reserve kept its main interest rate unchanged on Wednesday, citing risks to inflation and jobs from the ongoing Iran war, which has driven Brent crude oil to $119 per barrel. Fed Chair Jerome Powell highlighted economic uncertainty and solid growth, with four dissents on the statement's easing bias.

items.title: US National Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP
items.summary: The US national debt held by the public exceeded 100% of GDP for the first time since World War II in March, now at $38.97 trillion as of May 1. Projections indicate it could double over the next three decades due to increased spending.

items.title: Q1 GDP Grows 2%, Below Expectations
items.summary: US first-quarter GDP expanded at 2%, slightly under the forecasted 2.3%, driven by AI/computer equipment investment and consumer spending. This comes amid rising energy prices and war-related pressures.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge to $4.39 per Gallon Nationally
items.summary: The AAA national average for regular gas rose over nine cents in one day to $4.392, fueled by the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz crippling Iran's economy and spiking oil prices.

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rates Climb to 6.3%
items.summary: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate increased to 6.3% this week from 6.23%, according to Freddie Mac data released Thursday, amid persistent inflation and economic uncertainty.

items.title: Powell Pledges to Remain as Fed Governor Post-Term
items.summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell stated he will not retire after his term ends next month, committing to serve as a constructive governor without acting as a shadow chair.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Rebound as US CPI Data Looms
items.summary: Bitcoin trades at $78,318 (+2.31%) and Ethereum at $2,296.69 (+1.49%), rebounding amid high Treasury yields and ETF outflows, with US April CPI data expected to drive volatility today.

items.title: Bitcoin and Dogecoin Surge as Tether Profits Soar
items.summary: Bitcoin and Dogecoin lead gains with BTC at $78,345 (+2.63%) and DOGE at $0.1090 (+2.35%), fueled by Tether's strong profits, though dormant Ethereum wallets were drained and oil prices sparked a dip.

items.title: Ripple Unlocks Over a Billion XRP Worth $1.3 Billion
items.summary: Ripple released over a billion XRP tokens, injecting $1.3 billion into the market and putting traders on alert for potential price volatility amid fragile indicators.

items.title: Binance Records $216M Net USDT Inflow in 24 Hours
items.summary: Binance saw a $216 million net inflow of USDT, signaling potential boosts to exchange liquidity and broader crypto market activity.

items.title: US Debt Hits 100% of GDP for First Time Since WWII
items.summary: US national debt reached 100% of GDP, a level not seen since WWII, with CBO warning of 120% by 2036, amid ongoing crypto market movements.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below Key Holder Cost Basis Ahead of $1.74B Options Expiry
items.summary: Bitcoin's put-call ratio rose to 0.73 with dropping volume as it slips below holder cost basis, ahead of a massive $1.74 billion options expiry.

items.title: Big Tech Earnings Fuel Crypto Rally with Bitcoin Up 2.7%
items.summary: Big Tech earnings sparked a risk rally, pushing Bitcoin up 2.7% to near $78K in the past 24 hours, though weekly gains are modest at 0.6%.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 Delayed to May 2026
items.summary: Rockstar Games has delayed Grand Theft Auto 6 to May 26, 2026, delivering the anticipated but dreaded news to fans.

items.title: Nintendo Sues Alleged Switch 2 Leaker Genki
items.summary: Nintendo is taking legal action against accessory maker Genki for allegedly misleading consumers regarding Switch 2 information.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced for 2026 with PS5 Demo Now Available
items.summary: Nioh 3 is set for release in 2026, and players can try its demo on PS5 immediately.

items.title: Final Fantasy Tactics - The Ivalice Chronicles Launches September 2026
items.summary: A remaster of Final Fantasy Tactics titled The Ivalice Chronicles is scheduled for launch in September.

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Revealed Earlier Than Expected
items.summary: Silent Hill f is coming sooner than anticipated, as announced in recent showcases.

items.title: DC Comics Video Game Franchise Returns in 2026
items.summary: A new DC Universe video game has been confirmed via listing for a 2026 release.

items.title: Subnautica 2 Enters Early Access on May 14
items.summary: The highly anticipated Subnautica sequel will launch into early access on May 14 after significant development drama.

items.title: Elder Scrolls: Oblivion Remake Confirmed with Teaser
items.summary: Bethesda has officially confirmed the Oblivion remake with a cryptic teaser, hinting at a sooner-than-expected reveal.

items.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Releases October 6
items.summary: Star Wars: Galactic Racer is landing on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S on October 6, with a collector's edition available.

items.title: PlayStation State of Play Reveals Major Titles Including Digimon and Bloodstained
items.summary: June's PlayStation State of Play showcased Final Fantasy Tactics remaster, Silent Hill f date, Digimon updates, new Bloodstained game, and more.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Ubuntu and Canonical Servers Down After Sustained Cyberattack
items.summary: Servers operated by Ubuntu and its parent company Canonical have been offline for over a day due to a sustained, cross-border cyberattack. This disruption affects critical infrastructure for the popular Linux distribution.

items.title: Samsung Ordered to Pay ZTE $392M for Patent Infringement
items.summary: A UK court has ruled that Samsung must pay ZTE $392 million for patents essential for phone network access. Samsung faces additional similar lawsuits from ZTE in China, Germany, and Brazil.

items.title: Apple Discontinues Cheapest Mac Mini Model
items.summary: Apple has appears to have discontinued its entry-level Mac mini, with the compact desktop now starting at $799 and including 512GB of storage as standard.

items.title: Nissan Abandons Plans for US EV Plant
items.summary: Nissan has scrapped its plans to build an electric vehicle plant in the US, which was intended to produce 200,000 EVs annually.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.

items.title: Microsoft's Xbox Mode Rolls Out to Windows 11 PCs
items.summary: Microsoft is bringing its console-like Xbox mode to Windows 11 PCs, providing a unified gaming interface across platforms.

items.title: Copernicus Sentinel-1D Satellite Now Fully Operational
items.summary: The Sentinel-1D satellite, launched last November, has completed its in-orbit commissioning and is now live, completing Europe's radar mission with all four Sentinel-1 satellites deployed.

items.title: Oura Ring Adds Detailed Hormonal Health Insights
items.summary: Oura has introduced more comprehensive hormonal health tracking to its Series 3 and 4 rings, including monitoring potential side effects of various birth control methods.
